note: golden_hash filled by `node golden-parity.test.mjs --update`. Vectors cover: standard single-currency rounding, multi-currency holdings + computed accruals + MMF-style rounding, and the full rounding-mode matrix (half_up/half_even/truncate) at an exact .XX5 boundary — the case most likely to be silently wrong.

vectors.name: single-currency-standard-rounding
vectors.description: Two USD holdings, no accruals or liabilities, 2-decimal half_up rounding (standard fund convention).

not_proven.detail: Every price and FX rate is caller-supplied and asserted. This kernel performs no market-data lookups (zero-egress) and does not verify these values against any external source.
This is arithmetic recomputation over declared inputs, never an independent fair-value opinion or third-party valuation.
All inputs are point-in-time as supplied by the caller; this kernel makes no claim about market conditions at any other moment.
40-Act/UCITS references are informative context only; this kernel makes no claim of compliance with either framework's NAV-computation or filing rules.

output_payload.fence: Pricing/FX inputs are SUPPLIED, asserted, and digested into this receipt. This kernel recomputes the ARITHMETIC over those declared inputs and attests THAT — never a fair-value opinion, never a valuation, never live market data (zero-egress by contract).
output_payload.regulatory_framework: 40-Act/UCITS NAV-computation conventions referenced as informative context only; this kernel makes no compliance claim under either framework.

vectors.name: multi-currency-accruals-mmf-rounding
vectors.description: EUR holding converted via supplied FX, a principal/rate/day-count computed income accrual, a direct-amount expense accrual, a liability, and 4-decimal half_even (MMF-style) rounding.

not_proven.detail: Every price and FX rate is caller-supplied and asserted. This kernel performs no market-data lookups (zero-egress) and does not verify these values against any external source.
This is arithmetic recomputation over declared inputs, never an independent fair-value opinion or third-party valuation.
All inputs are point-in-time as supplied by the caller; this kernel makes no claim about market conditions at any other moment.
40-Act/UCITS references are informative context only; this kernel makes no claim of compliance with either framework's NAV-computation or filing rules.

output_payload.fence: Pricing/FX inputs are SUPPLIED, asserted, and digested into this receipt. This kernel recomputes the ARITHMETIC over those declared inputs and attests THAT — never a fair-value opinion, never a valuation, never live market data (zero-egress by contract).
output_payload.regulatory_framework: 40-Act/UCITS NAV-computation conventions referenced as informative context only; this kernel makes no compliance claim under either framework.

vectors.name: rounding-matrix-half-up-boundary
vectors.description: Rounding-mode matrix vector 1/3: net_assets/shares lands exactly on the 1.005 boundary at 2 decimal places. half_up rounds half away from zero -> 1.01.

not_proven.detail: Every price and FX rate is caller-supplied and asserted. This kernel performs no market-data lookups (zero-egress) and does not verify these values against any external source.
This is arithmetic recomputation over declared inputs, never an independent fair-value opinion or third-party valuation.
All inputs are point-in-time as supplied by the caller; this kernel makes no claim about market conditions at any other moment.

vectors.name: rounding-matrix-half-even-boundary
vectors.description: Rounding-mode matrix vector 2/3: same 1.005 boundary. half_even rounds to the nearest even digit -> 1.00 (0 is even, 1 is odd).

not_proven.detail: Every price and FX rate is caller-supplied and asserted. This kernel performs no market-data lookups (zero-egress) and does not verify these values against any external source.

vectors.name: rounding-matrix-truncate-boundary
vectors.description: Rounding-mode matrix vector 3/3: same 1.005 boundary. truncate drops all sub-cent precision -> 1.00.
